enonce.texte: Instructions: Match the restaurant vocabulary with their definitions.

questions.texte: Match the following terms:

sousQuestions.texte: 1. Menu 2. Waiter 3. Bill 4. Order 5. Tip
a. The amount of money you pay for the meal
b. The food or drink you ask for
c. The person who serves you in a restaurant
d. Extra money you give for good service
e. A list of food and drinks available

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Complete the restaurant dialogue with the correct words from the box.

Words: bill, order, menu, waiter, tip, table, dessert, soup

questions.texte: Read the dialogue and fill in the blanks:
questions.reponse: 1. table, 2. menu, 3. order, 4. soup, 5. dessert, 6. bill, 7. tip

sousQuestions.texte:
Waiter: Good evening! Welcome to our restaurant. Do you have a reservation?

Customer: No, we don't. Can we sit at that ________ (1) near the window?

Waiter: Of course. Here is the ________ (2). I'll be back in a few minutes to take your ________ (3).

Customer: Thank you. I would like the chicken with rice, and my friend wants the ________ (4) of the day.

Waiter: Excellent choices. Would you like something for ________ (5)?

Customer: Yes, we'll have the chocolate cake.

(After the meal)

Customer: Could we have the ________ (6), please?

Waiter: Certainly. Here it is.

Customer: Here is my credit card. And keep the change as a ________ (7).
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. table, 2. menu, 3. order, 4. soup, 5. dessert, 6. bill, 7. tip

enonce.texte: Instructions: Complete the sentences with the correct expression of purpose: to, in order to, so as to, for.

questions.texte: We went to the restaurant ________ have a nice dinner.
She studied the menu carefully ________ choose the best dish.
I am saving money ________ buy a new phone.
He called the restaurant ________ make a reservation.
They left early ________ avoid the traffic.
This knife is ________ cutting bread.

intitule: Exercise 4: Choose the Correct Purpose Expression

enonce.texte: Instructions: Choose the correct expression of purpose for each sentence.

questions.texte: I went to the market ________ buy some vegetables.

sousQuestions.texte: A. for  B. to  C. so as  D. so that

questions.texte: She woke up early ________ catch the first bus.

sousQuestions.texte: A. for  B. so as to  C. in order to  D. to

questions.texte: This machine is ________ cleaning floors.

sousQuestions.texte: A. for  B. to  C. so as to  D. in order to

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the text below and answer the questions that follow.

"La Cachette" is a popular restaurant in Yaoundé. It is known for its delicious Cameroonian cuisine. The restaurant is open from 11:00 am to 10:00 pm every day. Customers can choose from a variety of dishes including ndolé, poulet DG, and grilled fish. The prices are reasonable, and the service is excellent. Many people go there to celebrate special occasions or simply to enjoy a good meal with family and friends. The restaurant also offers a special menu for children. Because of its good reputation, it is often full, so it is advisable to book a table in advance.

questions.texte: What is the name of the restaurant mentioned in the text?
What types of food does the restaurant serve?
questions.reponse: Cameroonian cuisine including ndolé, poulet DG, and grilled fish.

questions.texte: Why do people go to this restaurant?
questions.reponse: To celebrate special occasions or to enjoy a good meal with family and friends.

questions.texte: What advice does the text give about this restaurant?
questions.reponse: It is advisable to book a table in advance because it is often full.

enonce.texte: Instructions: In each word, identify which syllable is stressed. Write the stressed syllable in capital letters.

questions.texte: restaurant

sousQuestions.texte: Which syllable is stressed in 'restaurant'?

questions.texte: menu

sousQuestions.texte: Which syllable is stressed in 'menu'?

questions.texte: dessert

sousQuestions.texte: Which syllable is stressed in 'dessert'?

questions.texte: waiter

sousQuestions.texte: Which syllable is stressed in 'waiter'?

questions.texte: delicious

sousQuestions.texte: Which syllable is stressed in 'delicious'?

enonce.texte: Instructions: Write a short dialogue (6-8 exchanges) between a customer and a waiter at a restaurant. Use at least 3 expressions of purpose and 5 restaurant vocabulary words.

questions.texte: Write your dialogue here.
questions.reponse: Students' answers will vary. Example: "Waiter: Good evening! Welcome to our restaurant. Would you like to see the menu? Customer: Yes, please. I came here to try your famous grilled fish. Waiter: Excellent choice. Here is our special menu for today. Customer: I would like to order the fish with vegetables. I also want to reserve a table for next week in order to celebrate my mother's birthday. Waiter: Of course. I will note that. Would you like something to drink? Customer: Yes, I will have some fruit juice to refresh myself. Waiter: Very good. I will bring your order soon. Customer: Thank you. I will leave a tip for good service."

sousQuestions.texte: List the restaurant vocabulary words you used.
sousQuestions.reponse: Students should list at least 5 vocabulary words (e.g., menu, order, waiter, reserve, tip, table, bill).

sousQuestions.texte: List the expressions of purpose you used.
sousQuestions.reponse: Students should list at least 3 expressions of purpose (e.g., to try, in order to celebrate, to refresh).

enonce.texte:
Instructions: You are the owner of a new restaurant. Write a short advertisement (6-8 sentences) to attract customers. Include the following information:

  • The name of your restaurant
  • The type of food you serve
  • The prices (reasonable, expensive, or cheap)
  • Why people should come to your restaurant (purpose)
  • Contact information (optional)

questions.texte: Write your restaurant advertisement here.
questions.reponse: Students' answers will vary. Example: "Welcome to 'Taste of Cameroon'! We serve delicious local dishes like ndolé, poulet DG, and fresh grilled fish. Our prices are very reasonable, and we offer special discounts for students. Come to our restaurant to enjoy authentic Cameroonian cuisine in a friendly atmosphere. We also have a children's menu. Visit us to taste the best food in town! Call us at 699 999 999 to book a table. Don't miss out on this wonderful experience!"

sousQuestions.texte: What expressions of purpose did you use in your advertisement?
sousQuestions.reponse: Students should identify expressions of purpose (e.g., to enjoy, to taste).

enonce.texte: Instructions: Each sentence has a mistake. Identify and correct it.

questions.texte: I went to the restaurant for eat with my friends.
questions.reponse: I went to the restaurant to eat with my friends.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain your correction.
sousQuestions.reponse: We use 'to + verb' to express purpose, not 'for + verb'. 'For' is followed by a noun or gerund.

questions.texte: We need a table for fifth people.
questions.reponse: We need a table for five people.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain your correction.
sousQuestions.reponse: We use numbers (five) for quantity, not ordinal numbers (fifth). 'Fifth' indicates position in a series.

questions.texte: He order the grilled fish.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain your correction.
sousQuestions.reponse: The verb 'order' should be in the past tense 'ordered' because it is a completed action.

questions.texte: She gave the waiter a tip for he had good service.
questions.reponse: She gave the waiter a tip because he had given good service.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain your correction.
sousQuestions.reponse: We should use 'because' to show the reason for giving the tip. Also, the verb tense should be consistent.
